---
title: "FengLab"
description: "Studying human immunity across age, infection, and disease through systems immunology and translational research."
page-layout: full
toc: false
body-classes: home-page
---

::: {.home-hero}
::: {.hero-glow aria-hidden="true"}
:::

::: {.hero-content}
::: {.eyebrow}
Systems immunology · Single-cell science · Human health
:::

# Decoding human immunity, one cell at a time.

We combine single-cell multi-omics, high-dimensional proteomics, computational biology, and translational research to understand how immune systems develop, respond to infection, and change across the lifespan.

::: {.hero-actions}
[Explore our research →](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[Meet the team](people.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg}
:::
:::

::: {.hero-aside}
::: {.orbit-mark aria-hidden="true"}
::: {.orbit orbit-one}
:::
::: {.orbit orbit-two}
:::
::: {.orbit orbit-three}
:::
::: {.cell cell-one}
:::
::: {.cell cell-two}
:::
::: {.cell cell-three}
:::
::: {.cell cell-four}
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.home-section .intro-section}
::: {.section-kicker}
Our approach
:::

## From molecular detail to human health

::: {.section-intro}
The immune system is dynamic, deeply individual, and shaped over a lifetime. We connect measurements across biological scales to reveal the principles behind that complexity.
:::

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-number}
01
:::

### Single-cell multi-omics

We integrate transcriptomic, epigenomic, proteomic, and immune-receptor measurements to characterize complex immune responses.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-number}
02
:::

### Human immunology

We study immune variation across individuals, developmental stages, clinical cohorts, and disease states.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
::: {.feature-number}
03
:::

### Infection & prevention

We investigate immune responses to infection, vaccination, and preventive interventions.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.home-section .mission-band}
::: {.mission-copy}
::: {.section-kicker}
Why it matters
:::

## Better maps of immunity can lead to better medicine.

By pairing rich human datasets with rigorous computation, we aim to discover biological patterns that can inform prediction, prevention, and treatment.
:::

::: {.mission-link}
[Discover our research programs →](research.qmd)
:::
:::

::: {.home-section .updates-section}
::: {.updates-card}
::: {.section-kicker}
From the lab
:::

## Follow our latest work

Read about new studies, laboratory milestones, and other updates from the FengLab.

[View lab news →](news.qmd){.text-link}
:::

::: {.updates-card .updates-card-accent}
::: {.section-kicker}
Join us
:::

## Do your best work with us

We welcome curious, collaborative researchers who want to advance our understanding of human immunity.

[Explore opportunities →](join.qmd){.text-link}
:::
:::
